programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: The history of the concentration camp, charting its origins as a place to hold political prisoners through its development into a mechanised factory for mass murder. The first episode features the accounts of Polish inmates held there prior to the Holocaust, Jewish survivors and a former SS officer, who discusses being placed in charge of a firing squad(n)

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: A look at the early stages of genocide at the camp, examining the role of commander Rudolf Hoess in the process. Despite later claiming he was only following orders, Hoess was instrumental in the development of Auschwitz for mass murder, arranging for Soviet prisoners to be brought in as construction workers and scouring Europe for more victims to kill. Featuring contributions from survivors(n)

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: Examining the complex system the Nazis set up to implement the extermination of the Jewish race throughout Europe from 1942, with Auschwitz as the hub. The programme reveals that at other camps, including Treblinka, Belzec and Sobibor, rival commanders participated in the genocide with enthusiasm and shared ideas for the best method of mass murder(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough presents an exploration of Earth's vast seas, which cover 70 per cent of the planet's surface. In the first episode, millions of squid appear off the coast of California, gannets, whale sharks and dolphins hunt migrating sardines in the Indian Ocean, and thousands of turtles make the annual pilgrimage to lay their eggs in Costa Rica(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Camera crews journey to the deepest parts of the world's oceans to explore the lives of their inhabitants, including fearsome predators with huge teeth, strange jellyfish and deep-sea sharks that lurk among the gigantic submerged mountains and volcanos. Presented by David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Predators and prey in the open oceans come under the scrutiny of David Attenborough. Marlin track down a shoal of sardines as a sei whale scoops up the leftovers, dolphins herd mackerel and attract the attention of a sailfish with the resonance of their sonar, and giant sunfish queue up to allow other species, including seagulls, to feed on their parasites(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Marine life at the poles, where winter temperatures frequently fall to a numbing -40C. Even when the sun reappears in the spring, its weak rays take a long time to melt the ice. David Attenborough describes how wildlife including seals, whales, polar bears and penguins overcome the elements to survive through prolonged cold spells(n)

programme.desc: Hand-drawn animated version of Judith Kerr's much-loved tale, which celebrated its 50th anniversary in 2018. A tea-guzzling tiger turns up unannounced and eats and drinks young Sophie and her mummy out of everything in the house, even draining the water from their taps(n)

programme.title: Big Fat Quiz of the Year 2019

In an attempt to find out, Jimmy Carr welcomes Dara O Briain, Maya Jama, Asim Chaudhry, Roisin Conaty, Richard Ayoade and Noel Fielding to the end-of-year celebrity panel game, putting their knowledge of the past 12 months to the test. programme.desc: Four contestants in York battle it out, with Svenja starting proceedings with her German take on British food, followed by charming waiter Oli, who pins his hopes on quality service and table manners. programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Four dinner hosts try to outdo one another in Milton Keynes, beginning with sales executive Becky, whose menu proves a little too cheesy for her guests. However, Columbo is unconvinced and draws on his broad palette of experience to reveal the truth. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Patrick Bauchau and Fionnula Flanagan(n)

programme.title: Columbo: Swan Song (1974)
programme.desc: A country singer arranges for his wife and his underage lover to both die in a phoney plane crash after his spouse diverts all his money to her religious causes in revenge for his affair. He is confident he remains above suspicion, but Columbo realises something does not add up and sets out to wrest a confession from the musician. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Johnny Cash and Ida Lupino(n)

programme.title: Columbo: Short Fuse (1972)
programme.desc: The son of a chemical company's founder is blackmailed by his uncle, who wants to force him into selling the business. The cunning heir responds by murdering his relative with a box of exploding cigars, but finds himself facing the dishevelled detective, who is convinced the resulting fatal detonation was no accident. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Roddy McDowall and Ida Lupino(n)

programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: Agatha Raisin
programme.desc: Ashley Jensen plays the title role in this quirky crime drama, based on the novels of MC Beaton. Bored with her new life in the Cotswolds, former London-based PR whizz Agatha Raisin re-doubles efforts to ingratiate herself with the local community. However, when entering its annual quiche-making competition, she inadvertently finds herself a suspect in a murder case. With her reputation tarnished and hopes of romance with a dashing ex-army neighbour seemingly thwarted, Agatha feels she has little choice but to clear her name by unmasking the real culprit. With Hermione Norris, Robert Bathurst, Katy Wix and Mathew Horne(n)

Meanwhile, Mrs Thackeray has one too many arguments with Mr Pritchard, prompting her to make a momentous decision that throws the running of the house into chaos(n)

programme.title: Churchill: Winning the War, Losing the Peace
programme.desc: A look at Winston Churchill's battle to be elected Prime Minister just weeks after VE Day. The war leader was confident of victory, but ended up being humiliated at the polls with the Conservative party almost annihilated. Surprising revelations from first-hand witnesses, including Sir Max Hastings, Juliet Gardiner, Anthony Beevor and Dave Douglas, help to uncover whether Churchill's rejection was a mark of ingratitude, or the most mature decision ever made by a democracy(n)

programme.title: Harry & Meghan: A Very Modern Romance
programme.desc: The story of Prince Harry and Meghan Markle's relationship, examining whether their marriage will radically change the British monarchy(n)

programme.title: The Kid From Canada (1958)
programme.desc: A boy visits his family in the Scottish Highlands, where his boastfulness gets him into trouble during a horse riding trip. Family drama, with Christopher Braden and Bernard Braden(n)

programme.title: The Riddle of the Sands (1979)
programme.desc: An English yachtsman sailing round the coast of Germany in 1901 encounters a naval officer who has betrayed his country. Enlisting the aid of a friend, he discovers a plan by Kaiser Wilhelm to invade Britain - which they are the only ones in a position to stop. Spy thriller, starring Simon MacCorkindale, Michael York and Jenny Agutter(n)

programme.title: Jungle Street (1960)
programme.desc: Two men commit a robbery, but when one of them takes the rap for it, the other muscles in on his girlfriend. Crime drama, starring Jill Ireland, David McCallum and Kenneth Cope(n)

programme.title: Upstairs Downstairs
programme.desc: The house is rocked by controversy when the publication of a scandalous novel exposes Blanche's unconventional lifestyle, bringing the doctor face to face with the author of the book and her former lover Lady Portia Alresford. But when the passion between the pair reignites, the doctor becomes conflicted. Meanwhile, Persie reveals a shocking secret to Hallam, and Beryl's workload prompts her to take a stand - until a familiar old face appears to offer a few wise words. Emilia Fox guest stars, with Alex Kingston, Jean Marsh, Ed Stoppard and Claire Foy(n)

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Watford Ladies
programme.desc: London Live's James Dickman joins Watford Ladies football team both on and off the pitch, to get first hand experience of life with the squad(n)

programme.title: Harry & Meghan: A Very Modern Romance
programme.desc: The story of Prince Harry and Meghan Markle's relationship, examining whether their marriage will radically change the British monarchy(n)

programme.title: The Hottest Day of Winter
programme.desc: An experimental visual reflection of the hottest winter day on record in the UK, which fell on February 26 2019(n)

programme.title: A Random Encounter (2013)
programme.desc: Two strangers trying to make their way in Hollywood meet by chance in a coffee shop, and come away unable to stop thinking about one another. However, they both soon discover that they have no way of tracking the other down. Romantic comedy, starring Abby Wathen, Michael Rady, Meghan Markle and Sean Young(n)

programme.title: A Song for Jenny
programme.desc: Drama, based on Julie Nicholson's book of the same name, which explores her response to her daughter's death at Edgware Road tube station in the July 7 bombings. The rollercoaster of emotions experienced by Julie and her family after the attack is explored; from hope, denial and acceptance, to grief and the desire to honour Jenny. The programme coincides with the forthcoming 10th anniversary of the 7/7 bombings in London and is a tribute to those who lost their lives in the attack(n)

programme.title: The Face of Love (2013)
programme.desc: A widow begins a new relationship with a man who is the exact double of her husband, and shares many of his personality traits. Over time, she grows to love his own unique qualities, but fears he will leave her if he ever discovers the resemblance. Romantic drama, starring Annette Bening, Ed Harris and Robin Williams(n)

programme.title: School For Sex (1969)
programme.desc: A fraudster hatches a plan to open his own finishing school, where women can learn how to use sex to take advantage of wealthy men. However, the sudden appearance of his students in high society raises the suspicions of the police and the media. Comedy, starring Derek Aylward and Rose Alba(n)

programme.title: Food Junkies
programme.desc: Shemzy selects his favourite moments from the series that cuts straight to the core of London's food scene(n)

programme.title: Upstairs Downstairs
programme.desc: The house is rocked by controversy when the publication of a scandalous novel exposes Blanche's unconventional lifestyle, bringing the doctor face to face with the author of the book and her former lover Lady Portia Alresford. But when the passion between the pair reignites, the doctor becomes conflicted. Meanwhile, Persie reveals a shocking secret to Hallam, and Beryl's workload prompts her to take a stand - until a familiar old face appears to offer a few wise words. Emilia Fox guest stars, with Alex Kingston, Jean Marsh, Ed Stoppard and Claire Foy(n)

programme.title: Churchill: Winning the War, Losing the Peace
programme.desc: A look at Winston Churchill's battle to be elected Prime Minister just weeks after VE Day. The war leader was confident of victory, but ended up being humiliated at the polls with the Conservative party almost annihilated. Surprising revelations from first-hand witnesses, including Sir Max Hastings, Juliet Gardiner, Anthony Beevor and Dave Douglas, help to uncover whether Churchill's rejection was a mark of ingratitude, or the most mature decision ever made by a democracy(n)

programme.title: Lusitania: 18 Minutes That Changed World War One
programme.desc: Docudrama about the sinking of British ocean liner RMS Lusitania 11 miles off the southern coast of Ireland by a German U-boat on May 7, 1915, which resulted in the deaths of 1,198 passengers and crew and was a key factor in the US's entry into the First World War. The events of that day are brought to life using archive footage, photographs, documents, dramatic reconstructions and expert opinion by marine historians, as well as first-hand testimonies by survivors and the submarine captain(n)

programme.title: Churchill: Winning the War, Losing the Peace
programme.desc: A look at Winston Churchill's battle to be elected Prime Minister just weeks after VE Day. The war leader was confident of victory, but ended up being humiliated at the polls with the Conservative party almost annihilated. Surprising revelations from first-hand witnesses, including Sir Max Hastings, Juliet Gardiner, Anthony Beevor and Dave Douglas, help to uncover whether Churchill's rejection was a mark of ingratitude, or the most mature decision ever made by a democracy(n)

programme.title: D-Day: The Shortest Day
programme.desc: Survivors provide a compelling insight into the D-Day landings, discussing the preparations, their actions, and the consequences of an operation which had such a crucial bearing on the outcome of the Second World War(n)

programme.title: Upstairs Downstairs
programme.desc: The house is rocked by controversy when the publication of a scandalous novel exposes Blanche's unconventional lifestyle, bringing the doctor face to face with the author of the book and her former lover Lady Portia Alresford. programme.title: D-Day: The Last Heroes
programme.desc: Historian Dan Snow presents the first of a two-part documentary examining how two years of meticulous planning, espionage and the analysis of millions of 3D aerial photographs helped the Allied forces gain a foothold in northern France during the Second World War. He reveals how the June 1944 landings were conducted in two phases, an airborne assault and an amphibious landing. The essential element of surprise was upheld with some help from inclement weather and a comprehensive deception plan(n)

programme.title: Friend or Foe (1982)
programme.desc: Two Second World War evacuees face a dilemma when they meet a German pilot who has been shot down. Family drama, starring John Bardon and Stacey Tendeter(n)

programme.title: Where Angels Fear to Tread (1991)
programme.desc: A wealthy widow travels to Italy to escape the pressure of her in-laws, and promptly falls in love with a poor local. When the revived woman agrees to marry her foreign friend, her relatives back home are shocked by the apparent disregard for class, culture and their family's image, and set about putting an end to the unlikely union. Period drama, based on the novel by EM Forster, starring Helena Bonham Carter, Judy Davis and Rupert Graves(n)

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Series featuring inspirational walks between from around Britain. Andrew White walks on a disused railway in the Peak District and alongside the Caledonian Canal(n)

programme.title: Upstairs Downstairs
programme.desc: Spargo signs up for a boxing tournament to impress Beryl, only to be told he is too old for the ring, so he recruits Johnny as his protege. However, he ends up teaching his young friend fighting skills of a different kind when he receives his call-up papers. Upstairs, the Hollands drift further apart, with Lady Agnes drawn to American millionaire Caspar Landry, who shows her an exciting world away from Eaton Place, while Sir Hallam becomes distracted by his desire for another woman - and makes a dangerous decision that will change his life for ever(n)

programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing lesser known areas of London for locals and tourists. Featuring Woolwich, Deptford and Lewisham. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay(n)

programme.title: Reservoir Dogs (1992)
programme.desc: Criminals are employed by a crime boss to carry out a diamond robbery that goes disastrously wrong. In the bloody aftermath of the heist, they try to establish what happened and how the police were aware of their plans all along - leading to the hardening suspicion that one of them betrayed the gang. Quentin Tarantino's thriller, starring Harvey Keitel, Tim Roth, Michael Madsen, Chris Penn and Steve Buscemi(n)

programme.desc: A retired couple face a grim future after a financial crisis costs them both their pensions. Refusing to take the situation lying down, they resolve to take back what is rightfully theirs with a series of daring bank robberies. Comedy, starring Bernard Hill, Virginia McKenna, Simon Callow and Sue Johnston(n)

programme.desc: Major Ken Hames examines events surrounding the 1980 Iranian Embassy siege in London, during which six Arab terrorists held more than 20 hostages at gunpoint until SAS forces stormed the building, freeing the captives and killing five of their captors(n)

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner(n)

While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Comedian and 2004 I'm a Celebrity winner Joe Pasquale, broadcaster Janet Street-Porter, TV presenter Mark Durden-Smith and former rugby union player Austin Healey pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a large cash prize for charity. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught. Bradley Walsh presents(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: TV presenter Dan Lobb, musical theatre star Jodie Prenger, wildlife expert Chris Packham and Coronation Street actress Jennie McAlpine (Fiz Stape) compete to win thousands of pounds for charity by pitting their general knowledge against one of the show's resident trivia experts. However, they must work as a team to build their prize pot and stay one step ahead of the Chaser. Presented by Bradley Walsh(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Journalist Matthew Wright, TV presenter Suzi Perry, Ian 'H' Watkins from Steps and actress Crissy Rock (Benidorm) compete to win thousands of pounds for charity by pitting their general knowledge against one of the show's resident trivia experts. However, they must work as a team to build their prize pot and stay one step ahead of the Chaser. Presented by Bradley Walsh(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Tenor Russell Watson, EastEnders star Rita Simons (Roxy Mitchell), wildlife presenter Bill Oddie and actress and broadcaster Denise Welch compete to win thousands of pounds for charity by pitting their general knowledge against one of the show's resident trivia experts. However, they must work as a team to build their prize pot and stay one step ahead of the Chaser. Presented by Bradley Walsh(n)

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Classic episode of the quiz show in which athletes Paula Radcliffe and Jonathan Edwards try to double their cash prize with each correct answer and win as much money as possible for their chosen charities. Next in the hot seat are ex-goalkeeper David Seaman and the show's first £1million winner Judith Keppel, hoping to scoop the jackpot again(n)

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Chris Tarrant hosts the first of two celebrity specials of the big-prize quiz, inviting a line-up of famous faces to have a go for charity _ with Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck. Joan Collins and her husband Percy Gibson attempt to raise money for The Shooting Star Children's Hospice, while Stephen Fry teams up with Nigella Lawson in a bid to benefit the Terrence Higgins Trust and the Royal Marsden Hospital's Head and Neck Cancer Research Trust(n)

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Chris Tarrant hosts the first of two celebrity specials of the big-prize quiz, inviting a line-up of famous faces to have a go for charity _ with Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck. programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Chris Tarrant invites another line-up of contestants to answer questions that could change their lives - with lifelines Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck(n)

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Chris Tarrant invites more well-known faces to pair up and play the big-money quiz for charity. Greg and Lucy Rusedski take the hot seat in aid of the Catholic Children's Society, while Alastair Campbell and Fiona Millar raise money for the Leukaemia Research Fund. Also appearing are Gloria Hunniford, hoping to win £1million for the Caron Keating Foundation, and Frederick Forsyth, supporting the Society for Stars(n)

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: New series of the big-prize quiz, beginning with the first of two celebrity specials. Former MP Edwina Currie joins Big Brother contestant and Conservative Party speech-writer Derek Laud to raise money for NCH and Dogs Trust. Then presenter Gloria Hunniford and author Frederick Forsyth attempt to win cash for the Caron Keating Foundation and the Society for Stars - a charity that helps children with cerebral palsy(n)

programme.title: Winter Wipeout
programme.desc: A magician, a trio dubbed 'the Don Squad' and a woman who shares the same name as comedienne Dawn French are among the contestants tackling Granny's House, the Travelator and the Big Red Balls in the elimination rounds. The remaining competitors then face the Ski Lift and Winter Blunderland, where they are whittled down to three for a record-breaking Wipeout Zone. Presented by Richard Hammond and Amanda Byram(n)
